name: amap
title: amap
description:
next-generation scanning tool for pentesters
AMAP stands for Application MAPper. It is a next-generation scanning
tool for pentesters. It attempts to identify applications even if they
are running on a different port than normal.
.
It also identifies non-ascii based applications. This is achieved by
sending trigger packets, and looking up the responses in a list of
response strings.

commands.name: amap6
commands.description: amap v5.4 (c) 2011 by van Hauser <vh@thc.org> www.thc.org/thc-amap

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use amap because next-generation scanning tool for pentesters.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use amap as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `amap [Mode] [Options] <target> <port/portrange> [<port> ...]` as documented upstream for amap.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair amap with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `amap --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install amap. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `amap [Mode] [Options] <target> <port/portrange> [<port> ...]`. next-generation scanning tool for pentesters.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: next-generation scanning tool for pentesters.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: next-generation scanning tool for pentesters.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install amap
Install with: sudo apt install amap
